pr.title: Cleanup statics in vi, match and link vi1202
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR #2588 split crowd sound-effect code out of melee/vi/vi1202 into a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx translation unit, updated split and symbol metadata, linked vi1202 as Matching in configure.py, and redirected fighter/game includes from vi1202.h to crowdsfx.h. The bot report shows +2036 linked-code bytes, 25 new matches under main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx, and expected apparent broken matches in main/melee/vi/vi1202 from moving those symbols to the new unit.

key_files.role: Original vi1202 source reduced after moving crowd SFX implementation out to the new sfx/crowdsfx unit.
evidence_refs: pr_body: matching vi1202 required a split for new crowdsfx.c
bot_report: vi1202 broken matches correspond to moved symbols

key_files.role: New translation unit containing the moved crowd SFX code and many matched functions formerly attributed to vi1202.
evidence_refs: diff: new file includes CrowdSFX_UnkStruct, un_80321900, fn_803219AC, un_80321A00, un_8032233C, etc.

key_files.role: New header for crowd SFX declarations/types used by fighter and game-mode callers.
evidence_refs: diff: include users switched from vi/vi1202.h to sfx/crowdsfx.h

key_files.role: Header cleaned up after crowd SFX declarations moved to sfx/crowdsfx.h.
Split metadata narrowed vi1202 ranges and added melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c ranges for text, bss, sbss, and sdata2.
evidence_refs: diff: added melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c .text 0x80321900-0x80322620 plus bss/sbss/sdata2 ranges

key_files.role: Symbol metadata changed several vi-area data/string/float symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels around the vi1202/crowdsfx split.
Build configuration changed vi1202 from NonMatching to Matching and introduced a new sfx MeleeLib containing crowdsfx.c as NonMatching.
evidence_refs: diff: Object(NonMatching, melee/vi/vi1202.c) changed to Object(Matching, melee/vi/vi1202.c)
diff: added MeleeLib("sfx (Sound effects)", Object(NonMatching, melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c))

key_files.role: Include updated to consume crowd SFX declarations from the new sfx header.
Include updated to consume crowd SFX declarations from the new sfx header.
Include updated to consume crowd SFX declarations from the new sfx header.
Include updated to consume crowd SFX declarations from the new sfx header.
Angle-bracket include updated to the new melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h path.
Adjacent vi source cleanup affected by this PR, with limited evidence in loaded slice.
evidence_refs: bot_report: vi1201v1 .data regression and un_8031FD18_OnEnter small unmatched regression

key_files.role: Adjacent vi source cleanup affected by this PR, with limited evidence in loaded slice.
evidence_refs: bot_report: vi1201v2 un_80320A40_OnEnter improved, .data slightly regressed

what_changed: Added src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c and src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h for crowd sound-effect code.

Shortened melee/vi/vi1202.c split ranges and added a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c split covering text 0x80321900-0x80322620, bss 0x804A2F08-0x804A2F38, sbss 0x804D7050-0x804D7054, and sdata2 0x804DE148-0x804DE160.
Changed configure.py so melee/vi/vi1202.c is Matching and introduced a new sfx MeleeLib with melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c as NonMatching.
Updated several consumers in fighter and gm code to include sfx/crowdsfx.h instead of vi/vi1202.h.
Adjusted symbols.txt around the split by changing several string/float symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels.
Bot report recorded 25 new matches in main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx and apparent broken matches in main/melee/vi/vi1202 for the same moved symbols.

smart_moves.text: The author treated crowd SFX as a separate logical unit rather than keeping it in vi1202 solely for historical address ownership.
evidence_refs: pr_body: required a split for a new crowdsfx.c file since it made logical sense
diff: added melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c split and source/header

smart_moves.text: After moving declarations to a new owner header, direct include users were updated from vi1202.h to crowdsfx.h instead of relying on stale vi ownership.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_FallSpecial.c, fighter.c, ftcommon.c, gm_16AE.c include changes

smart_moves.text: The split metadata, configure.py object ownership, and symbol metadata were changed together, keeping source movement aligned with build/link metadata.
evidence_refs: diff: configure.py adds sfx library and marks vi1202 matching

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching a unit reveals that a contiguous tail belongs to a different logical subsystem, a translation-unit split can be the right decomp step if split ranges, configure ownership, headers, and caller includes are updated together.
evidence_refs: pr_body: matching vi1202 required split for new crowdsfx.c
diff: vi1202 .text end moved to 0x80321900 and crowdsfx .text added 0x80321900-0x80322620
diff: configure.py marks vi1202 Matching and adds sfx crowdsfx object

decomp_lessons.lesson: Progress reports after a TU split may show 'broken matches' in the old unit and new matches in the new unit for the same functions; interpret those as relocation evidence rather than necessarily source regressions.
evidence_refs: bot_report: 23 broken matches under main/melee/vi/vi1202 for corresponding symbols

naming_conventions.convention: New subsystem file/header used the descriptive path/name crowdsfx for crowd sound effects, while many uncertain functions and globals retained address-style un_/fn_ names and x-offset struct fields.
evidence_refs: diff: functions such as un_80321900, fn_803219AC, un_8032233C and fields x0/x4/x8 remain address/offset style

naming_conventions.convention: Symbol metadata for local compiler literals/data in the split region used local @ labels rather than keeping broad global un_/lbl_ names.
evidence_refs: diff: un_804003A0 and lbl_804003C8 etc. changed to local @218/@316 etc.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Translation-unit split at 0x80321900 moved functions from vi1202 into a new crowdsfx object, allowing vi1202 to be marked Matching while crowdsfx carried the moved code.
evidence_refs: diff: configure.py vi1202 Matching and crowdsfx NonMatching

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Metadata cleanup converted some split-local strings/floats from global symbols to local @ labels to reflect local ownership around the new split.

The new crowdsfx.c contains narrow codegen steering artifacts such as a push/pop dont_inline block, PAD_STACK(8), and M2C_FIELD accesses; the loaded PR evidence shows their presence but does not include author explanation for each tactic.
evidence_refs: diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around un_80321C70 and un_80321CA4
diff: M2C_FIELD(mpLib_80458868, f32*, offsets) in boundary checks

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed linked code 42.41% (+0.05%, +2036 bytes), matched data 41.43% (-0.01%, -144 bytes), and linked data 35.01% (+0.02%, +192 bytes). It listed 25 new matches, mostly in main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx, and 23 broken matches mostly in main/melee/vi/vi1202 from the split.
No human review comments or review records are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c M2C_FIELD mpLib_80458868 accesses.
Search for later renames of CrowdSFX_UnkStruct fields x0 x4 x8 xC x10 x14 x18 x1C x20 x24 x28 x2C.
Search for later changes to un_80321C70 pragma dont_inline or PAD_STACK in un_8032233C.
Look for follow-up PRs that move crowdsfx.c from NonMatching to Matching or improve its data match.
Search path facts for melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c ownership and split ranges around 0x80321900-0x80322620.
Search for vi1202.h cleanup after crowd SFX declarations moved to crowdsfx.h.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, counts, changed files
notes: The loaded diff is truncated, so detailed contents for the tail of crowdsfx.c, vi1201v1.c, vi1201v2.c, vi1202.c, vi1202.h, and crowdsfx.h are incomplete.
There are no loaded human review comments, so reviewer intent and acceptance reasoning beyond merge state are not inferred.
The bot report supports match/progress effects, but no local build command, objdiff command, or manual validation notes from the author are loaded.
Presence of pragmas, PAD_STACK, and M2C_FIELD is documented, but the loaded evidence does not prove why each tactic was necessary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU split can convert old-unit broken matches into new-unit matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2588, moving the crowd SFX code from vi1202 into melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c produced bot-report broken matches in main/melee/vi/vi1202 and corresponding new matches in main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx. For split PRs, evaluate old-unit breakage against new-unit ownership before classifying it as a regression.
evidence_refs: pr_body: split introduced because matching vi1202 required it
bot_report: 25 new crowdsfx matches and 23 broken vi1202 matches

source_update_candidates.title: melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c introduced by PR #2588 as split from vi1202
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2588 introduced src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c and src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h. The splits metadata moved vi1202 .text end to 0x80321900 and assigned melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c .text 0x80321900-0x80322620, plus bss 0x804A2F08-0x804A2F38, sbss 0x804D7050-0x804D7054, and sdata2 0x804DE148-0x804DE160.
evidence_refs: diff: splits.txt vi1202 range changes and new crowdsfx ranges
source_update_candidates.reason: Path/split ownership fact appears directly supported by PR diff, but should be curated into path facts rather than accepted by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: vi1202 marked Matching while crowdsfx introduced as NonMatching
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2588 changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/vi/vi1202.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/vi/vi1202.c") and added a new MeleeLib "sfx (Sound effects)" containing Object(NonMatching, "melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c").
source_update_candidates.reason: Build ownership/status change is source-owned metadata and should be reviewed by curator before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Review split PR progress reports as ownership moves, not only regressions
source_update_candidates.text: For PRs that intentionally split a translation unit, apparent broken matches in the old object can be expected if the same symbols become matched in the new object. Review the old and new unit rows together before treating the bot report as a regression.
evidence_refs: bot_report: main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx new matches mirror main/melee/vi/vi1202 broken matches
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential review guidance supported by this PR, but it may be too narrow or already covered by existing split/data standards; curator should decide whether to generalize.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that each pragma, PAD_STACK, or M2C_FIELD in crowdsfx.c is accepted permanent style; the PR slice shows them but does not include verifier evidence for each tactic.
Do not promote semantic names for un_80321xxx functions or CrowdSFX_UnkStruct fields beyond the file/header name; most functions and fields remained address/offset-style in the diff.
Do not treat small data regressions in vi1201v1/vi1201v2 as intentional or acceptable beyond the bot report; no author explanation or reviewer discussion is loaded.
